steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence opens a parenthetical with an em dash: "Interpol—the International Criminal Police ______". A parenthetical opened by an em dash must be closed by a matching em dash. The blank must supply the end of the parenthetical, so the correct choice ends with an em dash.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Organization,': a comma cannot close an em-dash parenthetical. The sentence becomes "Interpol—the International Criminal Police Organization, in 1971" — the em dash is never closed. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Organization—': the em dash is properly closed, neatly bracketing the definition: "Interpol—the International Criminal Police Organization—in 1971." ✓
  • Choice C — 'Organization': no closing punctuation at all. The parenthetical bleeds into the rest of the sentence without being closed. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Organization:': a colon cannot close an em-dash parenthetical, and it would incorrectly make "in 1971" appear to follow as a list item. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
